title: Institutional Delegation Represents PCC at 35th COMEPP Convention
excerpt: Undergraduate students participated in a national business simulation alongside forty academic institutions.
content: Passi City College demonstrated high academic standards at the 35th COMEPP National Convention. The delegation participated in rigorous business modeling and strategic pitching competitions, engaging with peers from forty premier institutions. This initiative underscores our commitment to aligning student competencies with national entrepreneurship benchmarks.

title: Division of Computing Receives Top Honors at CineStep Festival
excerpt: The production 'Epektus' secured multiple distinctions, including Best Technical Direction and Cinematography.
content: The Division of Computing & Information Systems achieved significant recognition at the CineStep Film Festival. The project 'Epektus' was conferred the award for Best Short Film, with additional citations for Technical Direction and Cinematography. This success reflects the department's mastery of digital media and contemporary visual communication.

title: Inaugural Electronic Sports Colloquium Held During College Week
excerpt: Strategic competition took center stage as various units participated in the inter-departmental digital tournament.
content: The 2026 College Week featured the successful integration of a Digital Strategic Tournament. Students across all academic units engaged in a showcase of collaborative strategy and cognitive agility. This event highlights the evolving nature of competitive extracurricular activities within the modern collegiate environment.

title: Dr. Palmares Assumes Presidency of ALCOR VI Regional Council
excerpt: Passi City College designated as the host venue for the upcoming Regional Hinampang 2026.
content: The election of Dr. Janice Jennifer P. Palmares as President of the ALCOR VI Council marks a definitive era of leadership for Passi City College. In a concurrent development, the institution has been selected to host the Regional Hinampang 2026 this April, an event expected to welcome hundreds of academic and athletic delegates to our facilities.

title: Fiscal Literacy Symposium Conducted for Community Extension
excerpt: The Business Department facilitated a community-based seminar on economic management in Brgy. Imbang Grande.
content: The Division of Corporate & Fiscal Management (DCFM) fulfilled its social responsibility mandate through an outreach program at Barangay Imbang Grande. The symposium focused on fiscal discipline, budgeting, and small-scale economic governance, bridging the gap between academic theory and practical community application.

title: Dr. Palmares Delivers Keynote at PRO6 Security Training Inauguration
excerpt: Institutional head emphasizes the intersection of professional ethics and national security protocols.
content: Dr. Janice Jennifer P. Palmares presided as the keynote speaker for the inauguration of the PRO6 Basic Internal Security Operations Course (BISOC). Addressing 280 PNP personnel, she articulated the vital necessity of moral fortitude and ethical governance in the execution of internal security and public service duties.
